summary: Investment Portfolio Dashboard is a React + TypeScript PWA for tracking a personal investment portfolio (crypto + stocks) with live price fetching, live USD→NOK conversion, allocation visualizations, editing holdings (quantity and custom price overrides) for what-if analysis, and analytics including a portfolio performance time-series graph with automatic daily snapshots at 18:00 Norway time (CET/CEST). The app integrates with an ICP Motoko canister backend for persistence/auth (Internet Identity) and uses third‑party APIs for market/FX data with caching and default fallbacks; recent iterations include per-asset fetch status indicators, API-provider split (CoinGecko for crypto; Finnhub for stocks via API key), and a mobile PWA install UX.

existing_features.title: Portfolio dashboard aggregation (totals, allocation, conversions)
existing_features.description: Builds a unified PortfolioData object by combining persisted assets with live market prices and USD→NOK exchange rate, computing total value (USD/NOK), per-asset value, and allocation percentages with robust fallbacks to defaults.

existing_features.title: Live crypto and stock price fetching with fallback and caching
existing_features.description: Fetches crypto prices from CoinGecko and stock quotes from Finnhub, rate-limits stock calls, caches last known successful results in memory, and returns per-symbol PriceData including status and user-facing messages when falling back.

existing_features.title: USD/NOK exchange rate fetching with cached and default fallback
existing_features.description: Fetches USD base rates from ExchangeRate-API, caches successful rates for offline fallback, and exposes rate metadata (source/status/message/timestamp) to the UI.

existing_features.title: Immediate default portfolio rendering (offline-first UX)
existing_features.description: Provides hardcoded default assets, default historical data, and default exchange rate via React Query initialData and try/catch fallbacks so the dashboard renders instantly even without backend actor or API availability.

existing_features.title: Asset holdings view with filtering and allocation pie chart
existing_features.description: Renders a filtered list of assets (all/crypto/stocks), displays value and 24h change badges, and visualizes portfolio allocation using a Recharts pie chart.

existing_features.title: In-app portfolio editing (quantity and custom price override)
existing_features.description: Provides a modal editor for asset quantity and optional custom USD price override, persists updates to the backend via mutations, and invalidates/refetches relevant queries to reflect changes across the dashboard.

existing_features.title: Reset portfolio to default values
existing_features.description: Calls a backend reset method to restore the default asset set for the authenticated user and triggers full query invalidation/refetch; also clears the frontend historical-data in-memory cache.

existing_features.title: Historical portfolio value tracking with daily synchronization
existing_features.description: Fetches historical portfolio data from the backend, normalizes/sorts points, ensures a data point for today matches the current computed NOK total, and attempts to persist updates back to the backend; includes a cache-based safeguard against apparent backend data loss.

existing_features.title: Historical portfolio graph with fullscreen mode
existing_features.description: Displays historical portfolio value in NOK as a Recharts line chart with custom axis/tooltip formatting and a fullscreen dialog for detailed viewing.

existing_features.title: Synthetic 30-day asset price history and performance chart
existing_features.description: Generates deterministic synthetic 30-day historical price series per symbol and visualizes selected assets (BTC/ICP/SPX) in a multi-line chart; includes a tabular performance summary UI.

existing_features.title: Scenario analysis projections with adjustable growth rates
existing_features.description: Allows users to adjust annual growth rate assumptions for base/bull/bear scenarios and computes projected portfolio values across multiple time horizons, including per-asset 12-month projections.

existing_features.title: ICP staking monthly yield and compounding projections
existing_features.description: Calculates ICP staking yield using staked/unstaked split, APY, and a user-adjustable ICP price slider; projects monthly compounded yields for 12 months and shows USD/NOK conversions using the current exchange rate from the portfolio state.

existing_features.title: Risk metrics dashboard (mock analytics)
existing_features.description: Displays mocked portfolio risk indicators (volatility, Sharpe, drawdown, beta, VaR), a radar chart of risk factors, and an asset risk contribution table with formatted/typed numeric helpers.

existing_features.title: Market news section with sentiment and asset tabs (mock)
existing_features.description: Provides a tabbed news UI filtered by asset symbol with sentiment badges and a sentiment summary breakdown; currently driven by mock news items.

existing_features.title: Theme toggle and responsive UI system
existing_features.description: Implements dark/light mode toggling via next-themes, styling via TailwindCSS with an HSL token system, and shared UI primitives (cards, tables, dialogs, tooltips, alerts).

existing_features.title: API health/status indicators in the UI
existing_features.description: Shows a header-level badge (Live/Partial Live/Offline/Loading) based on live price and exchange-rate availability and per-asset status indicators (success/custom/fallback) with tooltips explaining the current source.

existing_features.title: PWA support (manifest, service worker, install UX)
existing_features.description: Includes a PWA manifest and a simple service worker caching strategy, plus a mobile-only install button that uses the native install prompt when available or shows manual iOS/Android installation instructions.

existing_features.title: Internet Identity authentication and backend actor management
existing_features.description: Provides an InternetIdentityProvider for login/logout and identity persistence; creates an authenticated or anonymous canister actor accordingly and initializes backend access control on actor creation, invalidating/refetching queries when identity changes.

existing_features.title: Backend access control (admin/user/guest roles)
existing_features.description: Implements role-based permissions in Motoko where the first initializer becomes admin, subsequent principals become users, and anonymous callers are guests; guards portfolio/profile mutation and admin-only endpoints accordingly.

existing_features.title: Backend per-user portfolio persistence and mutations
existing_features.description: Stores per-principal portfolios and profiles using OrderedMap; supports reading the caller’s portfolio, updating asset quantity, setting/clearing custom prices, appending historical data points, and resetting to defaults (authenticated users only).

existing_features.title: Backend public read-only endpoints for shared/default data
existing_features.description: Exposes public query methods for shared historical data, default assets, exchange rate/status, API status, last update time, and health checks to support fast loading and guest access (read-only).

existing_features.title: Backend HTTP outcall utility module and admin fetch endpoints
existing_features.description: Provides a reusable Motoko HTTP outcall module (GET/POST with transform) and admin-only endpoints that can request CoinGecko/Finnhub/ExchangeRate-API data (currently returning raw responses).

existing_features.title: Blob storage mixin and authorization utilities (Caffeine storage tooling)
existing_features.description: Includes a storage subsystem and mixin supporting authorized gateway principal updates, dead-blob listing/confirmation, certificate creation, and a cashier cycles refill flow guarded by authorization checks.

existing_features.title: Automatic daily portfolio-history append at 18:00 Norway time (CET/CEST)
existing_features.description: Adds a client-side scheduler (hook) that automatically appends a new portfolio-history data point once per day at 18:00 Norway time (CET/CEST), updating the historical graph accordingly and avoiding reliance on manual refreshes.

existing_features.title: Root-level failsafe rendering (ErrorBoundary + SafeAppShell + global error UI)
existing_features.description: Adds a root-level React ErrorBoundary and a defensive SafeAppShell render path so the app always mounts and shows a usable default UI (or a fatal error screen/banner) even when auth/actor/data hydration fails; includes global error capture hooks/components to surface and recover from initialization/runtime errors instead of rendering a blank page.

existing_features.title: Stop the continuous UI blinking/lag by eliminating any silent React Query invalidation/refetch loop that happens while authenticated (Internet Identity). Ensure that actor/identity initialization does not repeatedly invalidate and refetch all non-actor queries on an ongoing basis.

Make the portfolio rendering stable during background updates: while live prices and FX rates are refetching, keep displaying the last known successful portfolio computation instead of swapping back to fallback/default values mid-session.

Add lightweight diagnostics to verify the fix: log (or otherwise surface) a single, clearly identifiable reason whenever portfolio-related queries are invalidated/refetched due to authentication/actor changes, and ensure this does not spam continuously in normal operation.

new_feature_requests.summary: Allow editing all asset values (quantities/prices/etc.) and persist/save the changes so they remain after refresh/reload
Allow editing asset quantities and prices for what-if valuation and save/persist those edits (no editing of historical graph points; intended for single-user personal use)
Add fullscreen/expandable mode for the historical portfolio performance graph (click to open in a large/fullscreen frame) while keeping UI structure otherwise unchanged
Add a mobile-only PWA install/download button at the bottom of the page to help users add the app icon to their home screen (native install prompt when available, otherwise instructions)
Update default ICP holding breakdown to 8,500 staked and 7,000 liquid (and include both correctly in yield calculations)
Compute and display portfolio-level 24h change as a weighted aggregate of the assets’ 24h % changes (not a simple or incorrect global %), with correct up/down indicators
Fetch and use live USD/NOK exchange rate in all NOK portfolio calculations (totals, per-asset NOK values, and performance graph), with fallback to last-known/default on failure
Persist and append portfolio performance graph points automatically each day at 18:00 Norway time (CET/CEST) without duplicates, ensuring the graph’s last point equals the current summed total

Use CoinGecko exclusively for crypto live prices/% changes (ICP, BTC, SPX6900) and Finnhub exclusively for stock prices/% changes (BMNR, MSTR, SBET) with per-asset success/failure indicators and fallbacks

Fix continuous UI flicker/blinking/lag caused by a silent refetch/state loop (likely React Query invalidation/refetch toggling between fallback and live prices), especially when logged in; ensure live data replaces fallback without oscillation

architecturalNotes: Frontend uses TanStack React Query for all data fetching/mutations with configured staleTime/gcTime/refetchInterval and extensive initialData-based fallbacks to prevent blank screens.
Data aggregation pattern: usePortfolio builds derived portfolio state by merging backend assets + client-side live prices + exchange rate, then computes totals and allocations.
Price and FX fetching is implemented client-side in a dedicated priceService module with in-memory caching (cachedPrices/cachedExchangeRate) and per-asset status messaging (success/fallback/custom).
Authentication uses Internet Identity via @dfinity/auth-client; identity changes trigger actor recreation and invalidate/refetch of dependent React Query caches (useActor).
Backend is an ICP Motoko canister (actor InvestmentDashboard) using OrderedMap for persistent maps and a role-based access control module (admin/user/guest).
Backend distinguishes public read-only query endpoints (e.g., shared historical data, default assets, health check) from authenticated shared/update functions for integrity.
Historical portfolio chart uses Recharts and keeps an in-memory historicalDataCache to protect against unexpected backend data loss and to ensure 'today' matches the current computed total.
UI architecture is component-based using TailwindCSS + shadcn-style UI components, lucide icons, and next-themes for dark/light mode; charts use Recharts.
PWA support includes manifest.json, a basic service worker cache strategy, and a mobile-only install UX with manual instructions for iOS/Android.
Backend includes reusable modules for HTTP outcalls (with response transform) and a blob-storage mixin with authorization and cycles refill mechanisms (Caffeine storage tooling).
User requirement refinement: daily portfolio-history point should be added automatically at 18:00 Norway time (CET/CEST), not just 'once per day'.
User requirement refinement: edits are for asset quantities and prices only (what-if targets); historical performance points should not be user-editable.
User requirement refinement: persistence does not need multi-user semantics; intended for personal single-user usage (still may use existing auth/backend unless changed explicitly later).
Historical tracking now includes a dedicated client-side scheduler (useDailyHistoryAppend) to append a portfolio-history point at 18:00 Norway time (CET/CEST).
React Query provider setup was consolidated to avoid multiple QueryClientProvider instances and split caches.
Frontend configuration/API keys were moved toward centralized config/env-driven values instead of hardcoding secrets in source.
Stock live pricing provider switched/confirmed to Finnhub API (requires API token) while crypto pricing uses CoinGecko; UI shows per-asset fetch success/fallback status indicators with tooltips.
Historical performance graph uses Plotly with a true datetime x-axis for proportional spacing between dates; last point should be forced to equal the current summed portfolio total to avoid mismatch.
PWA UX includes a mobile-only install button to prompt Add-to-Home-Screen / install flow.

Planned fix for draft/preview white-screen regression: add a root-level ErrorBoundary/failsafe render path and defensive guards so default portfolio UI renders before any async auth/actor/data hydration; harden init flow around auth/canister calls and history parsing to prevent blank screens in draft.

App initialization is hardened with a root-level RootErrorBoundary + SafeAppShell pattern, plus global error capture/banner components, to prevent draft/preview blank-screen regressions when auth/actor/data hydration or history parsing fails.
A safer actor-access layer (useSafeActor) is introduced to guard canister calls during identity/actor transitions and route failures into global error state instead of breaking initial render.

Introduced a dedicated reactQueryKey helper/utility to ensure TanStack React Query queryKeys never contain BigInt/Principal/Nat values directly; all such values are normalized (e.g., to string) before inclusion in query keys/options.

Added custom ESLint rules in the build template (e.g., no-bigint-in-query-keys, require-internet-identity-provider) to prevent regressions around BigInt queryKey hashing and missing Internet Identity provider wiring.
knownIssues: Backend defaultHistoricalData uses date strings like "23/01/2024" while the frontend parser in useHistoricalPortfolioData expects "yyyy-MM-dd"; if backend data is used as-is, date parsing can produce Invalid Date/NaN timestamps and break sorting/graphing.
useHistoricalData generates synthetic 30-day price history (sine-based) rather than fetching real historical market data; performance charts are therefore demo/mock rather than accurate.

Backend admin HTTP outcall functions (fetchCryptoPrices/fetchStockPrices/fetchExchangeRates/updateAllPrices) return raw API responses and do not parse/apply results to update stored asset prices, priceCache, apiStatus, or exchangeRate automatically; backend caching fields appear largely unused by the frontend.
Backend historical data appends new points without deduplication or bounds; repeated daily updates can grow unbounded over time and may contain duplicates for the same date.
Backend portfolio mutation endpoints do not validate negative quantities/prices; frontend prevents negatives in UI, but backend accepts arbitrary floats if called directly.
Guest users calling getCallerPortfolio will trap due to access control; the frontend catches and falls back to defaults, but this will generate console errors/noise.

Potential field-name mismatch risk: backend defines Asset.type_ while frontend code expects asset.type (depends on generated bindings); if not mapped, asset filtering/type display can break for authenticated portfolios.

Draft/build sometimes renders as a blank white screen or gets stuck loading portfolio values (rendering failure despite fallbacks), indicating a regression in initial data loading/render guards that must be fixed.

Draft/preview still renders as a completely white page (no content) even after refresh and with no visible console errors; likely an init/render regression before React mounts. Needs a root-level failsafe (ErrorBoundary + default render guards) and hardened auth/actor/data hydration to prevent blank screens.

UI flicker/lag: dashboard appears to alternate between default fallback prices and attempted live updates, causing visible blinking/lag (likely repeated refetch/re-render loop or unstable API fetch results/failed updates).

UI flicker/lag persists continuously (not just during initial load): dashboard appears to constantly blink between default fallback prices and attempted live updates; occurs while logged in (Google/Internet Identity) and with no repeated errors visible in the browser console—suggests a silent refetch/render loop or unstable query invalidation causing frequent state swaps.
